notes: Evals emphasize the deterministic modes (sync, verify) which can be asserted objectively. init is interactive (AskUserQuestion) and produces subjective prose, so it is evaluated via the human-review viewer using a pre-seeded interview transcript rather than hard assertions. Fixtures are small repos of different stacks to prove generality; create them under the workspace before running.

evals.prompt: I just pulled the storage layer out of `core` into its own `storage` package and moved the `class VaultStore` and `class FileStore` definitions with it. Can you bring our MANIFESTO.md back in line with the code?
evals.expected_output: Skill runs in sync mode: runs manifesto_state.py diff + verify, finds the moved/missing enforcer anchors for the storage classes, updates the enforcer paths in the prose + lock to the new `storage` package, re-runs write, and ends with verify passing. Does not silently weaken any invariant. Old location may be noted in section 5.
files: fixtures/py-service-moved-storage/ (Python repo with an existing MANIFESTO.md + .manifesto/lock.json whose storage enforcers point at the OLD core path)

evals.prompt: Before I open this PR I want to know whether our architecture doc is still accurate — don't change anything, just tell me what's drifted.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs in verify (read-only) mode: runs manifesto_state.py verify, reports each enforcer as ok/moved/missing/ambiguous and any changed tracked files, and recommends sync if drift exists. Makes ZERO edits to any file (manifesto, lock, or code).
files: fixtures/ts-lib-drifted/ (TypeScript library with a MANIFESTO.md + lock where one enforcer symbol was renamed)

evals.prompt: Set up a MANIFESTO.md for this project. Here are my answers up front so you don't have to ask: it's a CLI that converts CSVs to typed Parquet; users are data engineers; the one hard rule is that schema inference is deterministic (same input always yields the same schema) and that's enforced by the `freeze_schema` test; we also value zero-config defaults but that's just a preference; skip the theory and operations sections.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs in init mode but, given the pre-supplied answers, skips redundant questions. Produces a root MANIFESTO.md from the template with a Vision section, ONE invariant (deterministic schema inference) carrying an enforcer anchored to the freeze_schema test, the zero-config item listed under Values (not Invariants), and no theory/operations sections. Writes .manifesto/lock.json and runs manifesto_state.py write so verify passes. Offers the hook but does not install without consent.
files: fixtures/csv2parquet-cli/ (small Python CLI repo with a tests/test_schema.py containing def test_freeze_schema, NO existing MANIFESTO.md)
